notes: Issue #18 follow-up, authored by Claude. PURE-WAYBACK recipe recipes/aus_gg_wayback.yml — the live gg.gov.au is unreachable (Akamai silent drop, ReadTimeout at 90s for bot and browser UA), so no live recipe and no wayback_to bound. Recipe-only, engine untouched. Probe --spread: 535 snapshots, title/text green, dates parsed 1996-08-22 .. 2004-10-14 (reaches FURTHER BACK than the CDX capture years suggest, because captures from 2002-2006 preserve content dated from 1996). WHICH PATH MATTERS: gg.gov.au has two archived trees and only one is speeches. (1) USED: /speeches/html/speeches/<yyyy>/<yymmdd>.html — ~454 captures of the GG's actual speech texts, content 1996-2006, covering Governors-General William Deane (1996-2001), Peter Hollingworth (2001-2003) and Michael Jeffery (2003-2008). (2) NOT USED: /about-governor-general/governor-generals-program/<slug> — despite being the far bigger capture set (~6000, 2019-2026) it is the GG's PROGRAM, i.e. a diary of engagements whose slugs are place names (canberra-act, cloncurry-queensland-julia-creek-queensland) with ~760-970 char notes, not speeches; harvesting it would flood the corpus with diary rows. A few genuine speeches do sit there (address-reply, first-voice-power-speech) so a tightly slug-filtered second recipe could be worth it later. DATE HANDLING: these 2000s pages have no date element and no heading markup; date comes from url_regex ONLY (/speeches/<yyyy>/<yymmdd>.html — year from the DIRECTORY, so the 2-digit yy is never ambiguous). A body-regex date fallback was tried and parsed all samples correctly but was deliberately REMOVED: a body regex takes the first date-looking string, so a speech quoting another date would be mis-stamped. Title is weak (<title> is a stamp like 'SPEECH 27-08-2000'); the cleaner's GPT pass should derive a better title/context from the body. link_pattern keeps only the html/ copy — the site also archives pdfs/, rtf/ and textonly/ mirrors of the SAME speeches which would otherwise duplicate every row. speaker_default omitted (three GGs span the range). SCOPE NOTE: the Governor-General is the ceremonial head of STATE; the PM (pm.gov.au) is the head of GOVERNMENT and is likely higher value — decide scope before a full run. country=Australia; source_url=http://www.gg.gov.au/speeches/html/speeches (via Internet Archive); source_name=gg.gov.au; source_type=official_gov; region=Oceania; iso3n=36; content_format=fulltext; leaders_covered=governor-general. FULL RUN pending (researcher gate).
